prompt: Comparing enums with == gives unexpected results. What is the bug?
choices: == works for all enum comparisons and equals() is redundant
Comparing an enum to a String with == is a compile error because the types are incompatible; use a.name().equals(s) to compare the enum's name to a String
valueOf always returns a new object so == between two Status values is always false
equals() must be overridden in enums to compare by value
explanation: Using == between an enum and a String is a compile-time type error — the compiler knows they can never be the same object. To compare an enum's name to a String, use a.name().equals(s). Note: == between two enum values of the same type is fine and idiomatic (enums are singletons).
